name: Joe Farrell
abstract: Joseph Carl Firrantello (December 16, 1937 – January 10, 1986), known as Joe Farrell, was an American jazz multi-instrumentalist who primarily performed as a saxophonist and flutist. He is best known for a series of albums under his own name on the CTI record label and for playing in the initial incarnation of Chick Corea's Return to For…

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Elvin Jones Trio
New York Jazz Repertory Company: Jazz With a Spanish Tinge
Newport Jazz Festival: New York Jazz Repertory Company
Tribute to Charles Mingus: Mingus Music

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I called up Jimmy Garrison, I called up Joe Farrell, and I knew him very well so we got a trio together
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gap Mangione
about_quotes.evidence: And then Frank Wess, Joe Farrell, Jerome Richardson... Great players. And of course, the thing that I love so much is the kind of respect that they show... we walked in there as kids with those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gap Mangione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: He says, 'I want you to join the band tonight, as of now.' He says, 'Joe’s leaving. You’re going to take Joe’s place.' ... It was you, Joe Farrell, Gene, and Elvin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Let’s see, Joe Farrell was in the band, Eddie Deal played guitar, Garnett Brown played trombone... So, we all go down to the beach
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: You know the saxes when I joined were Frank Hittner was playing baritone and Joe Farrell was playing tenor and Willie and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Tony Leonardi extensively. Joe Farrell is mentioned only by the interviewer as a separate entity ('Joe Farrell was there a while') and later the interviewer mentions 'His brother Joe' (referring to Tony Leonardi's brother, Joe Leonardi, not Joe Farrell). about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022
